steps.title: Identify the conclusion
steps.content: Mulder and Spellman conclude that invasive species (like P. aviculare) gain an advantage from delayed subfreezing temperatures more than native species (like V. edule) do.

steps.title: Match the supporting finding
steps.content:
Choice A directly supports this: when subfreezing onset was late, both species extended their leaf production, but the extension was much greater for the invasive P. aviculare than for the native V. edule — the precise differential advantage the conclusion claims.

  • B shows no variation (no advantage)
  • C describes typical years (not late-onset)
  • D shows similar gains for both (no differential advantage)
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
